goal: Add model selection support to the Codex adapter. In internal/provider/codex.go, the runStructured method currently calls codex exec without a --model flag. Change it to accept the model name from the role profile and pass it as --model to codex exec. In internal/provider/provider.go or wherever RunLeader/RunWorker/RunPlanner/RunEvaluator are called, pass the role profile's Model field to the adapter. The adapter should only add --model if the model string is non-empty and not one of the Claude model names (opus/sonnet/haiku). Add a helper isCodexModel(model string) bool that returns true for GPT model names or empty string. Run go build ./... and go test ./... to verify.

verification_contract.goal: Verify that Codex provider executions honor role-profile model selection without passing Claude shorthand models to `codex exec`, and that the repo still builds and tests cleanly.
scope: Provider unit tests for model flag behavior
Documentation alignment for Codex adapter capabilities
required_checks: Confirm Codex leader/planner/evaluator/worker paths use the resolved role profile model.
Confirm `isCodexModel` accepts empty and GPT-style model names and rejects Claude shorthand names for Codex flag injection.
Confirm test coverage asserts both `--model` inclusion and suppression cases.
Confirm build and test commands pass from the workspace root.
disallowed_actions: Do not change evaluator gate semantics.
Do not modify Claude adapter behavior unless required for compatibility.
Do not redesign provider/session interfaces beyond the minimal wiring needed for this feature.
Do not skip `go build ./...` or `go test ./...` verification.
verification_contract.notes: Prefer the minimal implementation pattern already used by `internal/provider/claude.go`: keep role-profile resolution close to the adapter entrypoints unless a broader provider API change is strictly necessary.

summary: The required implementation is complete and verified. Codex adapter entrypoints now use the resolved role-profile model, `runStructured` conditionally adds `--model` only for GPT-style/empty Codex models via `isCodexModel`, Claude shorthand models are suppressed, focused provider tests were added for inclusion/suppression behavior, docs were updated, and `go build ./...` plus `go test ./...` both passed from the workspace root.
leader_context_summary: The job state includes a succeeded implement step that directly covers the requested Codex adapter model-selection change, test coverage, documentation update, and required build/test verification.

steps.task_text: Implement the Codex adapter model-selection change end-to-end in D:\News\Business\AIOrchestrator. Update internal/provider/codex.go so runStructured accepts the resolved role-profile model and appends `--model <name>` to `codex exec` only when the model is non-empty and is a Codex/GPT model, not Claude shorthand (`opus`, `sonnet`, `haiku`). Add helper `isCodexModel(model string) bool` that returns true for empty string and GPT-style model names, and false for Claude shorthand names. In the provider wiring (`internal/provider/provider.go` or the minimal correct call sites), ensure RunLeader/RunWorker/RunPlanner/RunEvaluator pass the role profile Model field through to the Codex adapter, mirroring existing Claude-style role-profile resolution without redesigning interfaces more than necessary. Add focused unit coverage in internal/provider/provider_test.go for both `--model` inclusion and suppression cases. Update docs/IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md to reflect Codex per-role model selection support. Then run `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` from the workspace root and report results with changed files and any notable design choices. Do not change evaluator gate semantics or Claude adapter behavior beyond minimal compatibility.
steps.summary: Implemented Codex per-role model selection end-to-end without changing evaluator semantics or Claude behavior. `internal/provider/codex.go` now resolves the role-profile model at each Codex entrypoint, passes it into `runStructured`, appends `--model <name>` only for non-empty GPT-style models, and suppresses Claude shorthand values via `isCodexModel`. No `internal/provider/provider.go` change was needed because the minimal Claude-style pattern already resolves role profiles from `job` inside the adapter entrypoints. Added focused unit coverage in `internal/provider/provider_test.go` for `--model` inclusion across leader/planner/evaluator/worker, suppression for empty/`opus`/`sonnet`/`haiku`, and direct `isCodexModel` cases. Updated `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md` to note Codex per-role GPT model selection support. Commands run: `gofmt -w internal/provider/codex.go internal/provider/provider_test.go`, `go test ./internal/provider`, `go build ./...`, `go test ./...`. Non-blocking warnings encountered: PowerShell reported `Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1` execution-policy load warnings on shell startup, but all commands completed successfully with exit code 0.
